100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 9, 1926, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin faced growing tensions as labor strikes threatened the stability of the nation. In the United States, the aftermath of the 1926 flood in the Midwest continued to challenge communities, with efforts underway for recovery and rebuilding. Meanwhile, in Germany, political unrest surged as extremist factions gained traction amid the country’s ongoing economic struggles.
